meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no version control (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy for Students: Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing and version control with this advanced guide. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional, scalable student projects.
content.answer_block: Fixing no version control in SaaS pricing requires implementing a robust Git-based workflow alongside a modular pricing engine. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative deployment, you ensure your pricing logic is versioned, auditable, and decoupled from your core business logic, preventing catastrophic deployment errors.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of broken code, the silent graveyard of a project that had no version control. Every tweak to your SaaS pricing logic felt like walking a tightrope without a net, and now, the entire architecture is unstable. You are not just building an app; you are building your reputation as an engineer.

This is the threshold where students become architects. By integrating rigorous version control with a modular pricing engine, you transform your chaotic codebase into a professional-grade SaaS system. Let's move beyond 'just making it work' and start building systems that can scale to thousands of users.

steps.title: Initialize Version-Controlled Environment
steps.description: Establish a Git repository and define your project structure to ensure all pricing changes are tracked and revertible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust folder structure for a SaaS application including a /pricing directory, a .gitignore file, and a README that outlines a branching strategy for feature development.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial commit with project structure'

steps.title: Decouple Pricing Logic
steps.description: Move pricing calculations into a standalone module to ensure that version control changes to business logic don't break the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a modular service for SaaS pricing calculations that accepts user tiers as input and returns dynamic pricing objects. Use TypeScript interfaces for type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /add src/services/pricing.ts --message 'Refactor pricing into a decoupled service module'

steps.title: Implement Versioned Configuration
steps.description: Store pricing plans in JSON or YAML files tracked by Git, allowing for audit trails when prices change.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a versioned pricing_config.json structure that supports multiple tiers, currency support, and feature flag associations for different user segments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude /edit src/config/pricing_config.json --apply 'Update tier pricing to reflect new annual discount structure'

steps.title: Automate Testing for Pricing Logic
steps.description: Ensure every change to your pricing is validated by automated tests before it hits the main branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the pricing service that cover edge cases like currency conversion, discount code application, and tier upgrades.

steps.title: Deploy with Atomic Versioning
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate the deployment process, ensuring that your production environment always matches a specific Git commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script that validates the current Git hash before pushing pricing updates to the production database.

pro_tips: Always use feature branches for pricing experiments; never commit directly to main.
Use 'Git Hooks' to run your pricing unit tests automatically before every commit.
Store pricing history in a separate database table with valid_from and valid_to timestamps.
Never hardcode prices; always fetch them from your versioned config service.
Maintain a 'changelog.md' specifically for pricing updates to track business logic evolution.
resources: Git Best Practices for SaaS: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: After completing this, integrate a real payment provider like Stripe or LemonSqueezy, using your newly established versioning workflow to manage their webhook events.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to build scalable, professional software that stands up to real-world demands. Stop fearing your own code and start mastering the architecture that powers the next generation of SaaS. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
